draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer high-contrast layouts that separate navigation from work area
facade.label: Dark sidebar, light content
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark UI with bright content areas; test sidebar-centric layouts
facade.rejectImplies: Explore unified color schemes; test full-screen or minimal navigation approaches

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer high-contrast layouts that separate navigation from work area
evidence.implication: Explore dark UI with bright content areas; test sidebar-centric layouts

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scannable card layouts over dense contact tables for quick client overview
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: Visual hierarchy and whitespace matter more than max information density; likely to favor cards, tiles, or gallery views elsewhere
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes compact efficiency and data density; expects table structures with many columns; may want spreadsheet-like views for batch operations

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers value interface design that feels like working with a person, not a system — using warm colors, rounded corners, and conversational copy to reduce friction and emotional labor.
facade.label: Warm, human interface
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should prioritize human-centered design with warm color palettes, approachable language, and personality-driven interactions that make task management feel less transactional.
facade.rejectImplies: The CRM should lean toward clean, minimal, professional-neutral design that stays out of the way and lets data speak for itself — cooler tones and systematic efficiency.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to friendly, approachable voice over corporate formality—they want tools that feel like they're built by peers, not enterprise software companies
facade.label: Casual and warm
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational copy, warm color palette, emoji use, playful microinteractions, relatable error messages
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward polished professionalism, neutral palette, formal language, minimal personality, corporate credibility signals

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers expect client data to appear instantly when opening a contact, with no loading states or skeleton screens.
facade.axisTargeted: Snappy vs fluid — whether micro-interactions feel responsive (instant) or allow breathing room (staged reveal)
facade.label: Instant contact load
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and zero-latency feel; the CRM should prioritize perceived responsiveness over gentle transitions.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers staged animations and breathing room; data appearing all at once feels jarring; they'd rather see purposeful load sequences that guide attention.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers benefit from persistent navigation on the left with content displayed as individual cards rather than a continuous list, allowing quick jumps between clients/projects while scanning multiple items simultaneously.
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers stable, always-visible navigation that doesn't scroll away; values seeing multiple discrete items at once; likely wants a dashboard-style overview of clients or projects
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers tabs or full-width layouts; may want less screen real estate devoted to navigation; possibly values immersive focus on single items or streamlined, minimal chrome

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers gravitate toward approachable, non-intimidating interfaces that feel collaborative rather than corporate
facade.axisTargeted: Softness vs. sharpness in visual hierarchy
facade.label: Rounded corners, soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: Explore gentle, curved UI elements; soften the contrast established by dark/light split; test card-based layouts with depth
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers prefer clarity and efficiency over warmth; maintain crisp edges and flat design; refine the dark sidebar contrast without softening

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards with metrics/charts vs. simple lists and summaries—reveals how much visibility into numbers they need to feel in control
Whether user expects instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate transitions—reveals if they work fast-paced or prefer thoughtful, measured workflows
